title: Request Smuggling, Response Splitting, Parser Differentials, and Downgrades · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A protocol pairing room predicts byte, parser, state, ownership, and cleanup transitions before assembling the first multi-recipient parser-consistency threat model for request smuggling, response splitting, parser differentials, and downgrades. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of multi-recipient parser-consistency threat model while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and eviden…
objectives: Reject Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length and conflicting length signals, consume no ambiguous next message, and close the connection.
Reject or consistently handle bare line endings, whitespace before colons, obsolete folding, leading blank lines, and invalid request-line separators across every hop.
Compare raw and decoded targets, path separators, percent encodings, authority, query boundaries, and routing between policy and downstream recipients.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Clte Tecl Defense
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop protocol team handles case 5252 in a incident replay lab retaining minimized request-smuggling corpora. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject framing and control-byte differentials before a message crosses recipients; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then saturate one stream window while another remains independently serviceable. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Reject Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length and conflicting length signals, consume no ambiguous next message, and close the connection.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Clte Tecl Defense.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for reject transfer-encoding plus content-length and conflicting length signals, consume no ambiguous next message, and close the connection. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Clte Tecl Defense, keep Clte Tecl Defense connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

steps.title: Downgrade Consistency Gate · changed case · Workshop Downgrade Consistency Gate
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop protocol team handles case 5258 in a field-normalizing cache with an independent origin parser. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject framing and control-byte differentials before a message crosses recipients; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then block extra QPACK streams and fall back without removing HTTP/2 availability. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Test HTTP/2 or HTTP/3 to HTTP/1.1 translation, proxy chains, cache parsing, and backend normalization with the same adversarial corpus.
